stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
The image illustrates the same aeroplane flying at two different angles of attack (AoA), 2° and 12°, and a simplified representation of the streamlines in the 3D airflow around the wing. Assuming that the aircraft flies at the same speed and altitude but at the two different angles of attack shown, which of the angles of attack produces the most induced drag?

options.a: 12° AoA produces more induced drag, because of the greater pressure differential than at 2° AoA
options.b: 2° AoA produces more induced drag, because of the lower pressure differential than at 12° AoA
options.c: 12° AoA produces more total drag than 2° AoA, but less induced drag.
options.d: As the aircraft maintains the same speed, the induced drag is the same for both angles of attack.
